message:
fix(sse): strict SSE spec compliance — BOM stripping, CR/CRLF/LF line endings

Bring the SSE parser into full compliance with the W3C Server-Sent Events
specification for line ending handling and BOM processing:

BOM stripping:
- Strip UTF-8 BOM (U+FEFF) from the beginning of the first feed() call
  per the SSE spec requirement that a leading BOM must be ignored
- Track bom_checked flag to ensure one-time processing

Line ending handling:
- Replace memchr(b'\n') with memchr2(b'\r', b'\n') to detect all three
  line ending formats defined in the SSE spec: LF, CR, and CRLF
- Bare CR (\r not followed by \n): treat as line terminator, advance past
  the single byte
- CRLF (\r\n): treat as single line terminator, advance past both bytes
- CR at end of buffer: defer processing until more data arrives to
  correctly disambiguate bare CR from CRLF split across chunks
- Bare LF: unchanged behavior

This fixes parsing of SSE streams from servers that use CRLF (Windows)
or bare CR (legacy Mac) line endings, which previously caused event data
corruption or missed events.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
